evidence.correctness_and_freshness: Internally consistent API usage: betterAuth() config with socialProviders.google, magicLink plugin with sendMagicLink, toNextJsHandler, createAuthClient with magicLinkClient, authClient.signIn.magicLink and authClient.signIn.social — these match the documented better-auth surface. nextCookies() last-plugin rule noted. No version pinned but symbols self-consistent.
evidence.executability: Install line `npm install better-auth better-auth/next-js better-auth/plugins better-auth/client/plugins` is wrong — these are subpath imports of the single `better-auth` package, not separate npm packages; npm will fail. Also `database: { type: 'sqlite', filename }` is not a valid better-auth database config (expects a Database instance/adapter). Both require non-obvious fixes before code runs.
evidence.completeness: All six pieces present: install bash block, .env template with exact names, server lib/auth.ts, client lib/auth-client.ts, sign-in page + dashboard, Google credential acquisition steps with URL, and test bash commands. Env vars listed match those referenced in code.
evidence.citation_anchoring: Cites better-auth GitHub docs source (github.com/better-auth/better-auth/...) and mintlify mirror, plus third-party (medium, makerkit, prismagraphql). Sources are listed only at end, not anchored near code. No canonical better-auth.com URL but GitHub repo docs are official-equivalent.

rubric_notes: docs_unavailable=true so long-tail handling applied: internal consistency strong, version not pinned — capped CORRECTNESS at 4. Install command bug is the main executability hit.
